content.text:
<user_query>
Thoroughness: very thorough.
Workspace: C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq.
Task: Identify exactly where website scans and calibration orchestration happen in backend and frontend. Focus on backend routes and frontend hooks/components invoking scan endpoints/edge functions. Return:
1) Call graph from UI action -> API route -> edge functions
2) Which pages are crawled currently and depth limits
3) Where placeholders/generic fallback text are generated
4) Exact files/functions to edit for deterministic multi-page pipeline.
Include concise file-path references and key symbols.
</user_query>
Searching the codebase for website scans, calibration orchestration, crawl limits, and placeholder generation.

Here is a concise map of where website scanning and calibration orchestration live, with paths as absolute Windows paths under your worktree.

---

## 1) Call graph: UI → API → edge functions

### Primary path (agent calibration “website scan”)

| Step | Layer | Symbol / route | What it does |
|------|--------|----------------|--------------|
| 1 | UI | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\frontend\src\pages\CalibrationAdvisor.js` | Wires `useCalibrationState`; `WelcomeHandshake` / audit UI calls `cal.handleAuditSubmit` (and manual fallback). |
| 2 | Hook | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js` → `handleAuditSubmit` | Orchestrates the scan sequence (lines ~337–638 in the version read). |
| 3 | HTTP | `apiClient.put('/business-profile', …)` | Clears contaminated profile fields before scan. |
| 4 | Edge (proxied) | `callEdgeFunction` in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\frontend\src\lib\api.js` → `POST ${API_BASE}/edge/functions/{name}` | Does **not** call Supabase from the browser; hits the backend proxy. |
| 5 | Backend proxy | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\integrations.py` → `proxy_edge_function` (`/edge/functions/{function_name}`) | Allowlisted names forwarded to `https://<SUPABASE_URL>/functions/v1/{name}` with the user’s `Authorization` (or QA/dev service-role bypass). |
| 6 | Edge (order in `handleAuditSubmit`) | `scrape-business-profile` → `calibration-business-dna` → `social-enrichment` → parallel `deep-web-recon` + `competitor-monitor` → then `POST /calibration/enrichment/website` | Fast HTML prefill; main DNA extraction; social; recon/monitor; deep backend enrichment. |
| 7 | Backend enrichment | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\calibration.py` → `website_enrichment` (`@router.post("/enrichment/website")`) | Server-side crawl + Serper + `get_ai_response` (Trinity) + **additional** `_call_edge_function` calls (service role or inbound bearer): `deep-web-recon`, `social-enrichment`, `competitor-monitor`, `market-analysis-ai`, `market-signal-scorer`. |

**Mermaid (calibration scan):**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[CalibrationAdvisor.handleAuditSubmit] --> B[PUT /api/business-profile]
  B --> C[POST /api/edge/functions/scrape-business-profile]
  C --> D[POST /api/edge/functions/calibration-business-dna]
  D --> E[POST /api/edge/functions/social-enrichment]
  E --> F[parallel: deep-web-recon + competitor-monitor]
  F --> G[POST /api/calibration/enrichment/website action=scan]
  G --> H[_crawl_site_context + Serper + get_ai_response]
  H --> I[_call_edge_function: deep-web-recon, social-enrichment, competitor-monitor, market-analysis-ai, market-signal-scorer]
  I --> J[integrations.proxy_edge_function -> Supabase functions/v1]
```

**Edge function implementations (repo):**  
`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\supabase\functions\` — notable: `calibration-business-dna\index.ts`, `scrape-business-profile\index.ts`, `social-enrichment`, `deep-web-recon`, `competitor-monitor`, `market-analysis-ai`, `market-signal-scorer`, plus `calibration-psych`, `calibration-sync`, `biqc-insights-cognitive` used later in the flow (`triggerComplete` / `callEdge`).

---

## 2) Which pages are crawled and depth limits

### A) Calibration backend deep scan (`calibration.py`)

- **Homepage:** `_fetch_html_and_text(url)` (HTTP + `scrape_url_text`).
- **Follow-up pages:** `_crawl_site_context(seed_url, seed_html, max_pages=5)` → `_extract_internal_links` only keeps same-domain links whose path contains one of: `/about`, `/service`, `/solution`, `/industry`, `/case`, `/testimonial`, `/team`, `/contact`, `/news`, `/blog`; collects up to `limit = max_pages + 2` candidates, then fetches **`links[:max_pages]`** (5) with **~5000 chars text per page**.

### B) Edge function `calibration-business-dna` (Firecrawl multi-page)

- `scrapeWebsiteWithKeyPages`: fixed **`["", "/contact", "/about", "/about-us", "/services"]`** (same origin), merged up to **24k** chars.  
- `buildScanUrls` uses seeds `["", "/about", "/about-us", "/contact", "/services"]` (used elsewhere in the same file).

### C) Enterprise ingestion pipelines (separate API routes, not wired into `handleAuditSubmit` by default)

- **`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\ingestion_engine.py`:** `MAX_PAGES = 7`, `MAX_HTML_BYTES = 1_500_000`; homepage then internal links sorted by **`PRIORITY_PATHS`** (about, services, team, pricing, blog/news); only priorities **1–5**; blog priority 5 limited to one extra page; **`MAX_DEPTH = 2` is defined but not referenced** in this file (no recursive depth crawl in code).
- **`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\hybrid_ingestion.py`:** `MAX_PAGES = 7`, **`MAX_DEPTH = 2` appears only in the module docstring** — the symbol is unused in the body; crawl is still bounded by page count and bytes.
- **Routes:** `POST /api/ingestion/run`, `POST /api/ingestion/hybrid` (see `server.py` includes for `ingestion_engine_router` and `hybrid_ingestion_router`).

### D) DSEE (“structural exposure” scan)

- **`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\dsee.py`:** `pages_crawled=1` — **homepage only** (explicit comment in `compute_confidence` usage).

---

## 3) Where placeholders / generic fallback text are generated

| Location | Mechanism |
|----------|-----------|
| `useCalibrationState.js` | If `isWowSufficient(wow)` fails, sets `wow.what_you_do` to a fixed user-facing string: *"Unable to extract enough detail — please describe your business below."* (near the `setWowSummary(wow)` block). |
| `calibration.py` → `website_enrichment` (`action == "scan"`) | Builds a **deterministic `enrichment` dict** before/without reliable AI JSON: `_clean_business_name`, `_extract_sentence_with_keywords`, `_infer_target_market`, `_infer_competitors_from_results`, empty strings/objects for many fields; on AI failure: `ai_json = {}` and log *"deterministic scan"*; parse failure: *"deterministic fallback"*. Later blocks synthesize **`executive_summary`**, **`market_position`**, **`competitor_analysis`**, **`trust_signals`** (keyword tokens), **`cmo_executive_brief`**, **`website_health`**, merge **`_build_swot`**, **`_build_competitor_swot`**, **`_build_cmo_priority_actions`**, **`_build_intelligence_gaps`**. |
| `useCalibrationState.js` | Treats `ex.abn` / `ex.address` equal to **`'Not available from current data'`** as non-signals when merging identity. |
| `calibration-business-dna/index.ts` | **`EXTRACTION_PROMPT`** demands nulls when no evidence (no invention); Perplexity + Firecrawl supply content; deterministic **`extractIdentitySignals`** for regex-based signals. |
| `dsee.py` | Example: competitor page-count placeholder comment in SDD branch (`Placeholder — actual from SDD`). |

---

## 4) Exact files/functions to change for a deterministic multi-page pipeline

To make multi-page crawling **deterministic** and **consistent** end-to-end, these are the natural edit surfaces:

| Priority | File | Key symbols |
|----------|------|-------------|
| 1 | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\calibration.py` | `_extract_internal_links`, `_crawl_site_context`, `website_enrichment` (entire `action == "scan"` branch), helpers `_infer_*`, `_build_*`, `_build_intelligence_gaps` |
| 2 | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\supabase\functions\calibration-business-dna\index.ts` | `scrapeWebsiteWithKeyPages`, `keyPaths` / `buildScanUrls`, `scrapeWebsite` |
| 3 | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js` | `handleAuditSubmit`, merge logic for `deepEnrichment` vs edge data, `isWowSufficient` / wow fallback string |
| 4 | Optional unify crawl | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\hybrid_ingestion.py` → `hybrid_crawl` + `POST /ingestion/hybrid`, or `ingestion_engine.py` → `run_extraction` + `POST /ingestion/run` — then **call from `website_enrichment`** instead of duplicating `_crawl_site_context` |
| 5 | Edge proxy allowlist | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\knq\backend\routes\integrations.py` → `EDGE_PROXY_ALLOWLIST` if new edge functions are added |
| 6 | Shared edge caller from backend | `calibration.py` → `_call_edge_function` (already used by `website_enrichment`) |

**Note:** `MAX_DEPTH` in `hybrid_ingestion.py` / `ingestion_engine.py` is currently **not implemented as recursive link depth**; a deterministic pipeline should either implement real depth tracking or remove the misleading constant/docstring.

---

### Quick reference: entry components

- Calibration UI entry: `CalibrationAdvisor.js` + `useCalibrationState.js`.  
- Backend scan API: `calibration.py` → `/enrichment/website`.  
- Client → edge: `api.js` → `callEdgeFunction` → `integrations.py` → Supabase `functions/v1`.
